name: python-fastapi
description: Python FastAPI development with uv package manager, modular project structure, SQLAlchemy ORM, and production-ready patterns.
capabilities: Generate modular FastAPI project structure with feature-based architecture
Create SQLAlchemy 2.0 async models with Pydantic v2 schemas
Set up uv package manager for fast Python dependency management
Implement authentication patterns with JWT and password hashing
Configure async database sessions with PostgreSQL and SQLite for testing
Generate Alembic migration files for database schema changes
author: 0xkynz
securitySummary: Static scanner flagged patterns from documentation examples including connection strings and shell commands. All findings are false positives - the skill contains template code for FastAPI development with no malicious intent or actual credential exposure.
valueStatement: Building Python APIs requires modern patterns for async handling, database integration, and project organization. This skill provides production-ready FastAPI templates using uv package manager, SQLAlchemy ORM, and feature-based architecture for scalable API development.
limitations: Does not generate frontend code or web UI components
Does not provision cloud infrastructure or container configurations
Does not include authentication provider integration beyond basic JWT
Does not generate GraphQL APIs or WebSocket handlers

useCases.targetUser: Backend developers building new Python API services
useCases.title: Create a new FastAPI backend service
useCases.description: Scaffold a complete FastAPI project with async SQLAlchemy, configuration management, and layered architecture for production deployment.

useCases.targetUser: Developers extending FastAPI applications with database integration
useCases.title: Add database models to existing FastAPI project
useCases.description: Generate feature modules with repository pattern, services layer, and Pydantic schemas for CRUD operations.

useCases.targetUser: DevOps engineers establishing Python development standards
useCases.title: Set up testing and development workflow
useCases.description: Configure pytest with async test clients, in-memory SQLite databases, and development commands using uv package manager.

promptTemplates.title: Create basic FastAPI project
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a new FastAPI project structure using uv package manager with async SQLAlchemy 2.0. Include configuration with pydantic-settings, database connection, and a basic health check endpoint.
promptTemplates.scenario: Starting a new FastAPI API project from scratch

promptTemplates.title: Add user authentication module
promptTemplates.prompt: Add a complete auth feature module to the FastAPI project with JWT token creation, password hashing using bcrypt, login endpoint, and protected route dependency.

promptTemplates.title: Create CRUD API for resource
promptTemplates.prompt: Generate a feature module for a "items" resource with repository pattern, service layer, Pydantic schemas, and full CRUD endpoints (list, get, create, update, delete).
promptTemplates.scenario: Building REST API endpoints for a data model

promptTemplates.title: Set up async database testing
promptTemplates.prompt: Configure pytest with async test client, in-memory SQLite database, fixtures for database sessions, and example integration tests for the user service.
promptTemplates.scenario: Setting up testing infrastructure for FastAPI application

bestPractices: Use feature-based modular architecture to organize code by domain rather than technical layer
Implement async/await throughout the application using SQLAlchemy async sessions and asyncpg
Separate concerns: routes handle HTTP, services contain business logic, repositories handle data access
Use Pydantic v2 for all request/response validation with ConfigDict for ORM integration
antiPatterns: Avoid mixing business logic in route handlers - delegate to service layer
Do not use synchronous database calls in async FastAPI endpoints
Avoid tight coupling between features - use dependency injection for cross-cutting concerns
Do not hardcode secrets or connection strings in source files - use environment variables

faq.question: What is the uv package manager and why use it?
faq.answer: uv is a fast, Rust-based Python package manager that replaces pip and pipenv. It offers significantly faster dependency installation and better caching. This skill uses uv for all Python tooling.

faq.question: How does the feature-based architecture work?
faq.answer: Instead of organizing files by type (all models together, all routes together), feature-based architecture groups all code for a feature in one directory. Each feature has its own api, schemas, services, models, and utils.

faq.question: What database support is included?
faq.answer: The template supports PostgreSQL with asyncpg for production and SQLite with aiosqlite for testing. SQLAlchemy 2.0 async patterns are used throughout for non-blocking database operations.

faq.question: How is authentication implemented?
faq.answer: Basic JWT authentication is demonstrated with password hashing using bcrypt. The skill includes utilities for token creation, password hashing, and protected route dependencies using FastAPI Depends.

faq.question: Can I use this for GraphQL APIs?
faq.answer: This skill focuses on REST APIs. For GraphQL, you would need to add Strawberry or Ariadne separately. The SQLAlchemy models and Pydantic schemas can be reused with a GraphQL framework.

faq.question: How do I run tests?
faq.answer: Tests use pytest with httpx AsyncClient for testing async endpoints. The conftest.py includes fixtures for in-memory SQLite database and async session management. Run tests with 'uv run pytest -v'.
